study_name: Phase 1 IVT PCV-25 in Healthy Adults (CVIA 096)

notes: No results posted on ClinicalTrials.gov. Data extracted from publication Tables 4a and 5a. Formulation A: 2.2 mcg per serotype (4.4 mcg for 6B) with 125 mcg aluminium phosphate.

study_name: Phase 2 IVT PCV-25 Dose-Ranging in Healthy Adults (CVIA 105)
publication_reference: Langley JM et al. Vaccine 75 (2026) 128236. doi:10.1016/j.vaccine.2026.128236
arms: PCV25 Formulation A (n=39), PCV25 Formulation B (n=57), PCV25 Formulation C (n=79), PCV20 (n=39)
notes: No results posted on ClinicalTrials.gov. Data extracted from publication Tables 4b-d and 5b-d. Formulation A (2.2/125): 2.2 mcg + 125 mcg alum. Formulation B (2.2/250): 2.2 mcg + 250 mcg alum. Formulation C (4.4/250): 4.4 mcg + 250 mcg alum. PCV20 control shared across formulation comparisons.

study_name: Phase 3 Study of 14-valent PCV (PNEUBEVAX 14) in Healthy Indian Infants
publication_reference: Matur RV et al. Vaccine 42 (2024) 3157-3165. doi:10.1016/j.vaccine.2024.03.056
tables_extracted: Figure 2b (IgG GMC), Table 2 (OPA GMT), Table 4 (6A cross-protection)
arms: BE-PCV-14 (n=641 GMC / n=192 OPA), PCV-13 (n=626 GMC / n=194 OPA)
notes: Not on ClinicalTrials.gov (registered at CTRI India). Phase 3 in 6-8 week old Indian infants. 3+0 schedule (6-10-14 weeks). Includes 22F and 33F unique to BE-PCV-14 and 6A cross-protection from 6B. No individual CIs reported for GMC or OPA values.

study_name: Immunogenicity and Safety Study of the 13-valent Pneumococcal Polysaccharide Conjugate Vaccine (PCV13-TT, Walvax) in 2-71 Months Old Healthy Infants and Toddlers
publication_reference: Zhao Y, Li G, Xia S, et al. Immunogenicity and Safety of a Novel 13-Valent Pneumococcal Vaccine in Healthy Chinese Infants and Toddlers. Front Microbiol. 2022;13:870973. doi:10.3389/fmicb.2022.870973
tables_extracted: Table 4 (IgG GMC, after infant series and after booster), Table 5 (OPA GMT); Table 3 (IgG % >=0.35 responders), Table 5 % OPA >=1:8 responders (used to derive individual-group CIs)
notes: Registered on ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT02736240) but resultsSection currently returns hasResults:false / no posted results as of 2026-07-20 re-check, so API extraction is not possible. Verified against manuscript: serotype 4 GMC after infant series (PCV13-TT=4.51, PCV7=7.79) matches CSV exactly. Manuscript reports only a between-group ratio 95% CI for GMC comparisons and no CI at all for OPA GMT -- no individual-group CI exists in source for either assay. All 104 CSV rows for this trial initially had 0/0 CI placeholders corrected to NA (2026-07-20). UPDATE (2026-07-20, continued): per user request, derived individual-group 95% CIs for 63 of the 104 rows using a log-normal back-calculation: Table 3 (IgG, GMC rows) and Table 5 (OPA, GMT rows) report the % of subjects reaching the WHO/assay threshold (IgG >=0.35 ug/mL; OPA >=1:8) at matching N alongside the GMC/GMT in Table 4/5. Assuming individual titers are log-normal: sigma_ln = (ln(threshold) - ln(GMC)) / Phi^-1(1-p), SE = sigma_ln/sqrt(N), 95% CI = exp(ln(GMC) +/- 1.96*SE). This is a DERIVED/ESTIMATED CI, not a value reported in the source -- distinct from a directly-transcribed CI. Left as NA (not derived) for 41 rows where the method is not well-posed: 36 rows where % responders = 100% or 0% (undefined SD), 4 rows with an internally inconsistent sign (reported %<50% while GMC>threshold or vice versa -- produces a negative/inverted SD), and 1 row where % responders is within ~2 points of 50% (SD blows up because Phi^-1 is near zero there, giving a nonsensical CI spanning 4 orders of magnitude). Full row-by-row log, including which rows were derived vs left NA and why: data/ci_lognormal_derived_2026_07_20.csv. Also found and fixed a separate, unrelated pre-existing bug while cross-checking against Table 5: 10 of 13 serotypes' OPA/PCV7/'after infant series' rows had the PCV13 GMT value duplicated into the PCV7 row instead of PCV7's actual value; corrected outcome_overview_value for those 10 rows from the manuscript table. Also fixed outcome_overview_time_frame_weeks, which was 0 for all 104 rows (both '1m post primary child' and '1m post boost child') in violation of project convention; corrected to 4. Participant counts (N) backfilled from Table 3/4/5 for all 104 rows.

study_name: Phase 2 Trial of 20-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine (20vPnC, Pfizer B7471003) Given With 13vPnC in Healthy Infants
data_source: manuscript (OPA only; GMC sourced from ClinicalTrials.gov API)
publication_reference: Senders S, Klein NP, Lamberth E, et al. Safety and Immunogenicity of a 20-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Healthy Infants in the United States. Pediatr Infect Dis J. 2021;40(10):944-951. doi:10.1097/INF.0000000000003277 (PMC8443440)
tables_extracted: Table 3 (OPA GMT, 13 matched serotypes) and Table 4 (OPA GMT, 7 additional PCV20 serotypes) - both are numeric data tables with 95% CIs, not figures
notes: CORRECTED 2026-07-20: a prior audit pass on this same date had misattributed this trial's OPA source to Simon MW et al. Hum Vaccin Immunother. 2023 (doi:10.1080/21645515.2023.2245727) - that manuscript's own trial registration is NCT03550313, a different (open-label, cPCV7 Coadministered/Separated/Control) Pfizer trial not in this dataset, confirmed via the ClinicalTrials.gov API. It was likely mixed up because both trials test the same non-PCV13 serotype panel (8, 10A, 11A, 12F, 15B, 22F, 33F) and share Pfizer authors. The correct source, verified by NCT match via the ClinicalTrials.gov API and by exact match of all 80 pre-existing point values in the CSV against the manuscript's Table 3/Table 4 (0 mismatches), is Senders et al. 2021 PIDJ. Table 3 gives OPA GMTs for the 13 PCV13-matched serotypes (Doses 3 and 4, PCV20 and PCV13 groups); Table 4 gives the 7 additional PCV20-only serotypes (same structure) - together spanning exactly the 80 OPA rows (4 dose/group combinations x 20 serotypes) already in the CSV. Unlike the Simon/NCT03550313 paper, these are genuine numeric tables with 95% CIs, not figure error bars - no digitization was needed. All 80 rows backfilled from NA to real bounds (2026-07-20); see outcome_overview_ci_source column and data/ci_audit_2026_07_17.md for detail. Archived as data/publications/Senders 2021 PIDJ (PMC8443440).xml (full-text JATS XML; the publisher PDF was not reachable - blocked by a bot challenge - and NCBI's FTP mirror returned 404s for this article's PDF/tarball).

study_name: Immunogenicity and Safety of a Booster Dose of PCV13 in Children Primed With PHiD-CV or PCV13 (Czech Republic and Slovakia)
publication_reference: Urbancikova I, Prymula R, Goldblatt D, Roalfe L, Prymulova K, Kosina P. Immunogenicity and safety of a booster dose of the 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine in children primed with the 10-valent or 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine in the Czech Republic and Slovakia. Vaccine. 2017;35(38):5186-5193. doi:10.1016/j.vaccine.2017.07.103
tables_extracted: Supplementary Table 1 (OPA GMT with 95% CI, all serotypes/groups/timepoints) and Supplementary Table 3 (IgG GMC with 95% CI, all serotypes/groups/timepoints)
arms: Czech Republic 3+1 schedule: PHiD-CV-primed (n=53), PCV13-primed (n=45); Slovakia 2+1 schedule: PHiD-CV-primed (n=39), PCV13-primed (n=50)
notes: RESOLVED a block of 324 CSV rows (lines 4118-4441) that previously had every trial-identifying field blank, because the source has no ClinicalTrials.gov registration (EudraCT only) and the extraction pipeline had no CT.gov metadata to populate those fields from. Confirmed via exact value match on 3 sample rows (serotype 19A OPA month 12 PCV13/3+1 = 355.8 (219.9-575.6); serotype 1 OPA month 1 PHiD-CV/3+1 = 287.7 (213.7-387.3); serotype 5 OPA month 0 PHiD-CV/3+1 = 21.2 (13.6-33.1) - all exact). User supplied Supplementary Tables 1 and 3 (data/publications/Urbancikova_supplement.docx) which contain the full per-serotype-per-group-per-timepoint GMT/GMC values with real 95% CIs. All 324 rows backfilled 2026-07-20: 0/0 CI placeholders replaced with real bounds (0 unmatched, 0 value mismatches across all 324 rows), participant counts (N) filled in from the tables, and trial metadata populated (study name, sponsor, phase, country - split by schedule: 3+1 rows attributed to the Czech EudraCT registration/CZ, 2+1 rows to the Slovak EudraCT registration/SK). Also fixed a separate bug found in this block: outcome_overview_time_frame_weeks was 0 for all 324 rows regardless of timepoint; corrected 1-month-post-booster rows to 4 and 12-months-post-booster rows to 52 (pre-booster rows correctly stayed at 0).
